title: Add status watch primitive
description: Live dashboards and external aggregators need a coherent, read-only status feed that combines backend reachability, exposed and loaded models, route outcomes, warnings, recommended actions, and change information. Prototypes can poll `doctor`, `models`, and `route`, but the product should own aggregation and change detection instead of leaving every renderer to invent its own.
acceptance_criteria:
- `inferctl status --json` emits a full aggregate snapshot.
- `inferctl status --json --watch` emits repeated full snapshots at a configurable interval.
- Snapshot data covers backend reachability, exposed and loaded models, latest route outcomes for known task classes, warnings, and recommended actions.
- Change-event support is defined only after the snapshot model is stable.
- A Bubble Tea dashboard consumes the same status feed external tools consume instead of using a private aggregation path.
- The status surface remains read-only and does not run inference, warm models, load models, or create a hidden request path.
notes:
2026-06-30:
COMPLETED:
- inferctl-cox.1 defined the committed status snapshot schema.
- inferctl-cox.2 implemented inferctl status --json aggregate snapshots.
- inferctl-cox.3 added status --json --watch with configurable interval and graceful shutdown.
- inferctl-cox.4 defined snapshot-derived change event batches for backend reachability and route selection changes.
- inferctl-cox.5 added a Bubble Tea dashboard that consumes the public status --json --watch --events feed.
- Verified the series with go test ./..., scripts/check-contract-goldens.sh, git diff --check, CLI help checks, schema export checks, and focused status/dashboard/contract tests.
IN_PROGRESS:
- none.
NEXT:
- none.
BLOCKERS:
- none.
close_reason: Completed status watch primitive epic: status schema, status --json snapshots, status --json --watch with interval and graceful shutdown, snapshot-derived change events, and Bubble Tea dashboard consuming the same public status feed external tools use. Implementation commits: f515eac, cb53d3a, c82785a, df7ba55, ed6e16b. Verified go test ./..., scripts/check-contract-goldens.sh, git diff --check, help/schema checks, and focused status/dashboard tests.
agent_context: {"constraints":["status is read-only and aggregates the same non-inference probes used by doctor, models, and route","status must not warm models, ping by prompt, run benchmarks, perform sample inference, load models, or build hidden retention","watch mode emits polling-shaped snapshots or documented events without establishing a data-plane request path"],"preferences":["keep the Bubble Tea dashboard on the same status feed external tools consume","stabilize full snapshots before committing change-event records"]}

title: Define status schema
description: The status feed needs a committed aggregate envelope before implementation can safely add snapshots, watch mode, change events, or a TUI consumer. Without a documented schema, renderers and external aggregators would pin against accidental output shapes.
design: Define a status envelope that includes backend reachability, exposed models, loaded models, latest route outcomes for known task classes, warnings, and recommended actions. Document field meanings and schema versioning, then add a representative golden snapshot under `testdata/contract/`.
acceptance_criteria:
- Status snapshot schema is documented.
- The schema includes backend reachability.
- The schema includes exposed and loaded model information.
- The schema includes latest route outcome data for known task classes.
- The schema includes warnings and recommended actions.
- A golden snapshot for the committed schema exists under `testdata/contract/`.
notes:
2026-06-30 CLOSED:
COMPLETED:
- Added status.golden.json with version, summary, backend reachability, exposed/loaded models, route outcomes, warnings, and recommended_action.
- Documented the status snapshot schema in the contract goldens README.
- Added contract tests that require the status golden and validate required aggregate sections.
VERIFICATION:
- jq empty testdata/contract/status.golden.json passed.
- go test ./internal/contract -run 'TestStatusGoldenCoversAggregateSchema|TestVerbGoldens' passed.
- scripts/check-contract-goldens.sh passed.
- go test ./... passed.
BLOCKERS:
- none.
close_reason: Defined the status snapshot schema with contract docs, golden fixture, and aggregate field tests — commit f515eac

title: Emit status snapshots
description: Tooling needs `inferctl status --json` to return one coherent live-state snapshot using the committed status schema. This gives dashboards and external aggregators a product-supported source of truth before watch mode or event streams are layered on.
design: Implement `inferctl status --json` as a read-only command that aggregates the same non-inference probes already used by `doctor`, `models`, and `route`. Use the committed status schema and add contract coverage for representative backend states.
acceptance_criteria:
- `inferctl status --json` is registered in the CLI.
- The command emits an envelope matching the committed status schema.
- A contract golden for `status --json` passes.
- Integration coverage verifies the command against all five supported backend kinds.
- The implementation does not run inference, warm models, load models, mutate config, or proxy data-plane requests.
notes:
2026-06-30:
COMPLETED:
- Implemented read-only inferctl status --json aggregate snapshot command.
- Registered CLI verb, capabilities entry, schema reference, generated docs, and status golden.
- Added focused golden test and integration coverage across Ollama, llama.cpp, OpenAI-compatible, LM Studio, and MLX backend kinds.
- Verified go test ./..., scripts/check-contract-goldens.sh, and git diff --check.
- Implementation commit: cb53d3a Implement status snapshot command.
IN_PROGRESS:
- none.
NEXT:
- Close inferctl-cox.2 and continue with status watch loop.

close_reason: Implemented read-only inferctl status --json aggregate snapshot command in cb53d3a, including CLI registration, schema/capabilities/docs updates, status golden coverage, and all-five-backend integration tests. Verified go test ./..., scripts/check-contract-goldens.sh, and git diff --check.

title: Add status watch loop
description: Dashboards need a long-running `inferctl status --json --watch` mode that repeatedly emits complete status snapshots without duplicating aggregation logic in the renderer.
design: Layer watch mode on top of the snapshot emitter. Make the polling interval configurable, stream full snapshots, and handle process shutdown cleanly. Use existing probe capabilities without establishing a request path or executing inference.
acceptance_criteria:
- `inferctl status --json --watch` emits full status snapshots repeatedly.
- The polling interval is configurable.
- SIGTERM and SIGINT cause graceful shutdown.
- A 1-hour soak does not leak file descriptors or goroutines.
- Watch mode reuses the same aggregation path and schema as one-shot `status --json`.
notes:
2026-06-30:
COMPLETED:
- Added inferctl status --json --watch using the same snapshot aggregation path as one-shot status.
- Added configurable --interval with validation and graceful context/SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown via signal.NotifyContext.
- Updated capabilities/docs to advertise --watch and --interval.
- Added command-level tests for repeated newline-delimited JSON envelopes and cancellation plus non-positive interval rejection.
- Verified go test ./..., scripts/check-contract-goldens.sh, git diff --check, and status help.
- Implementation commit: c82785a Add status watch loop.
IN_PROGRESS:
- none.
NEXT:
- Close inferctl-cox.3 and continue with status change events.
BLOCKERS:
- none.
close_reason: Implemented inferctl status --json --watch in c82785a with configurable --interval, graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM/context shutdown, shared snapshot aggregation, capability/docs updates, repeated JSON envelope tests, and interval validation. Verified go test ./..., scripts/check-contract-goldens.sh, git diff --check, and status help.

title: Define status change events
description: Once full status snapshots are stable, dashboards need concise change records such as backend reachability changes and route switches. Defining events too early would freeze the wrong abstraction, but after snapshots stabilize the product can provide a cleaner narration layer.
design: Define diff/event records for meaningful status transitions, including backend offline/online changes and route switches. Emit events alongside snapshots under an explicit flag or through a second documented stream, while keeping the full snapshot as the baseline source of truth.
acceptance_criteria:
- Change-event schema is documented.
- Events can represent backend reachability transitions.
- Events can represent route selection changes such as primary-to-fallback switches.
- Events are emitted alongside snapshots under an explicit flag or documented second stream.
- Event output is derived from status snapshots rather than from a separate hidden probe path.
notes:

COMPLETED:

- Defined status event batch, backend reachability event, and route selection event payloads.
- Documented event schema and exposed status_event_batch in schema/capabilities.
- Added tests for backend reachability transitions, route primary-to-fallback selection changes, event-batch envelopes, and --events validation.
- Verified go test ./..., scripts/check-contract-goldens.sh, git diff --check, status help, and schema export for status_event_batch.
- Implementation commit: df7ba55 Define status change events.

Verified go test ./..., scripts/check-contract-goldens.sh, git diff --check, status help, and schema export.

title: Consume status in TUI
description: The Bubble Tea status dashboard should consume the same read-only `status --json --watch` feed external tools consume. A private aggregation path inside the TUI would make the dashboard behavior drift from the public tool-builder contract.
design: Wire the TUI to consume the documented status watch feed and render from that data. Remove or avoid renderer-local aggregation of doctor, models, and route outputs once the status feed is available.
acceptance_criteria:
- The Bubble Tea TUI reads status data from `inferctl status --json --watch`.
- The TUI does not maintain a separate private aggregation path for doctor, models, and route data.
- Dashboard change narration is sourced from documented snapshot or change-event fields.
- The TUI remains compatible with the same feed external tools can consume.
notes:
2026-06-30:
COMPLETED:
- Added Bubble Tea-backed inferctl dashboard command.
- Dashboard launches and reads the public inferctl status --json --watch --events feed as a subprocess.
- Dashboard parser consumes documented status snapshot and status event batch envelopes; it does not call doctor/models/route aggregation directly.
- Registered dashboard in root command, capabilities, generated verb docs, and tests.
- Verified go test ./..., scripts/check-contract-goldens.sh, git diff --check, dashboard help, focused dashboard/status tests, and contract tests.
- Implementation commit: ed6e16b Add status feed dashboard.

Verified go test ./..., scripts/check-contract-goldens.sh, git diff --check, dashboard help, and focused dashboard/status/contract tests.

title: Productize control-plane readiness verbs
description: inferctl needs first-class command verbs for readiness checks, comparable state snapshots, and domain-specific state diffs. Demo scripts can compose existing commands, but they cannot provide a stable product contract for automation, CI, evaluation harnesses, or agents. The durable behavior belongs in inferctl so examples remain thin and the CLI owns its policy decisions.
acceptance_criteria:
- inferctl exposes product-supported surfaces for preflight readiness, control-plane state snapshots, and control-plane state diffs.
- The new surfaces remain strictly control-plane only and never run chat, completions, embeddings, benchmark prompts, quality evals, sample inference, model warmup, or model loading.
- Prompt content is never persisted or emitted by default; commands that inspect prompts use metadata such as source, character count, estimated token count, hash, and filename or basename when relevant.
- New verbs are reflected in CLI registration, capabilities contract, schema export, generated verb docs, and contract goldens where applicable.
- The design does not introduce a top-level report verb, and route explanation remains part of the existing route command surface.
notes:
2026-06-30 CLOSED:
COMPLETED:
- Shared readiness contracts: prompt metadata, control-plane snapshot, change classifier, exit-code constants, schema definitions, and docs (inferctl-d1f.1, commit 31dbccd).
- Preflight readiness surface: inferctl preflight with metadata-only prompt handling, runnability policy, Markdown output, and contract coverage (inferctl-d1f.2, commit 6b1eb59).
- File-to-file diff: inferctl diff --before/--after with validated snapshot inputs and ranked domain changes (inferctl-d1f.3, commit 9bd5f0e).
- Snapshot artifacts: inferctl snapshot with raw comparable artifacts, prompt redaction, backend/model state, and diff compatibility (inferctl-d1f.4, commit d83310d).
- Retained history: snapshot --store with explicit storage and retention plus diff --task/--since lookup (inferctl-d1f.5, commit 82f74f3).
VERIFICATION:
- go test ./... passed.
- scripts/check-contract-goldens.sh passed.
- All child issues are closed.
BLOCKERS:
- none.
close_reason: Readiness verbs epic complete: shared contracts (31dbccd), preflight (6b1eb59), diff (9bd5f0e), snapshot (d83310d), and retained history/--since lookup (82f74f3); go test ./... and contract golden checks passed

title: Define shared readiness contracts
description: The new readiness verbs need a common contract for control-plane-only behavior, prompt metadata, output envelopes, schema versions, and reusable comparison policy. Without shared contracts, each command risks inventing incompatible JSON shapes, privacy behavior, and policy interpretation.
design: Identify the existing CLI registration, capabilities, schema export, generated verb documentation, and contract golden paths used by current commands. Define shared data shapes for route decisions, prompt metadata, backend reachability, loaded and installed model summaries, warnings, errors, inferctl version, contract version, and snapshot schema version. Keep the shared layer read-only and reusable by preflight, snapshot, diff, and any future TUI/status surface.
acceptance_criteria:
- Shared control-plane data types cover task, prompt metadata, route decision, route candidates, backend reachability, loaded and installed model summaries, warnings, errors, inferctl version, contract version, and snapshot schema version.
- Prompt metadata excludes prompt text and supports source kind, character count, estimated token count, optional content hash, and filename or basename when relevant.
- The contract preserves inferctl's exit-code taxonomy: 0 for runnable/success, 1 for invocation or policy blocks, 2 reserved for safety blocks, 3 for config or environment errors, and 4 for retryable transient control-plane failures.
- Shared comparison/classification code is structured so `inferctl diff` and a future TUI/status surface can reuse the same transition-ranking policy.
- Contract docs or generated artifacts make clear that the new verbs are read-only control-plane operations.
notes:
2026-06-30 CLOSED:
COMPLETED:
- Added shared prompt metadata with redacted file-source output and optional content hashing.
- Added shared control-plane snapshot and classified change data shapes for preflight, snapshot, diff, and future status surfaces.
- Added named exit-code constants matching the public taxonomy.
- Routed existing route prompt parsing through the shared metadata helper while preserving route output compatibility.
- Added schema definitions and agent-guide readiness contract documentation.
- Focused cmd/inferctl and internal/contract tests passed.
BLOCKERS:
- none.
close_reason: Added shared readiness prompt metadata, control-plane snapshot, and change-classification contracts; route prompt parsing now reuses the shared helper — commit 31dbccd

title: Implement preflight command
description: Automation needs one command and one exit code that answers whether the current inferctl control-plane state allows a task attempt. Existing commands answer related questions separately: doctor answers health, route answers selection, and triage ranks next actions. Preflight should own the product policy that decides when automation may continue.
design: Add `inferctl preflight <task>` with prompt-source handling aligned to route for the MVP surface: no prompt, `--prompt-file`, `--prompt`, `--from-stdin`, and the stdin marker where consistent with route. Build the result from doctor/route/triage-equivalent control-plane data without issuing sample inference, warming models, loading models, or persisting prompt content. Support JSON and Markdown output from the same decision data.
acceptance_criteria:
- `inferctl preflight <task>` is registered in the CLI, capabilities contract, schema export, generated verb docs, and contract goldens.
- The command accepts no prompt, `--prompt-file`, `--prompt`, `--from-stdin`, and the stdin marker where consistent with route; multiple prompt sources are rejected as invocation errors.
- `preflight --json` emits a standard inferctl envelope whose data includes requested task, prompt metadata, route decision, runnability status, applied policy flags, warnings, and recommended action.
- Prompt content is not emitted, persisted, or included in Markdown output.
- Exit codes are 0 when automation may attempt the task, 1 for invocation or policy blocks, 3 for config or environment errors, and 4 for retryable transient control-plane failure.
- `--allow-fallback` and `--require-ready` affect only runnability policy; they do not change route selection and do not warm, load, or query a model.
- `--format markdown` produces a PR/CI-friendly summary from the same control-plane decision data as JSON output.
- Tests cover primary ready, fallback accepted, fallback blocked, selected model not ready with `--require-ready`, unknown task, unreadable prompt file, invalid config, and no route available.
notes:
2026-06-30 CLOSED:
COMPLETED:
- Implemented inferctl preflight with task positional, optional stdin marker, prompt-file, inline prompt, stdin, fallback policy, readiness policy, and Markdown output.
- JSON output includes requested task, prompt metadata, route decision, route candidates, constraints, runnability status, policy flags, warnings, and recommended action.
- Policy failures emit data with an error envelope and exit 1; config/prompt/environment failures preserve exit 3; no-route failures preserve exit 4.
- Added capabilities, schema export, generated verb docs, error catalog, and contract golden coverage.
- go test ./... passed before close.
BLOCKERS:
- none.
close_reason: Added inferctl preflight with metadata-only prompt handling, runnability policy flags, Markdown output, JSON data on policy failure, and contract/docs/tests — commit 6b1eb59

title: Implement file-to-file diff
description: Drift debugging needs a domain-specific explanation of what changed between two known control-plane states. Generic JSON diff is too noisy and cannot rank route changes, fallback status, backend reachability, readiness, warning/error codes, or recommended actions in product terms.
design: Add deterministic file-to-file comparison for snapshot artifacts with `inferctl diff --before <snapshot.json> --after <snapshot.json>`, plus JSON and human output modes. Validate both inputs as comparable snapshot artifacts before classification. Rank domain-specific control-plane changes ahead of generic JSON churn and keep timestamp-only changes ignored or low priority. Defer relative-date lookup until snapshot storage and retention policy exist.
acceptance_criteria:
- `inferctl diff` is registered in the CLI, capabilities contract, schema export, generated verb docs, and contract goldens.
- The MVP supports deterministic file-to-file comparison with `--before` and `--after`.
- `--since yesterday` and other relative-date lookup forms are not part of the MVP unless snapshot storage and retention policy are implemented.
- The command emits a clear invocation or environment error for missing, unreadable, malformed, or incompatible inputs.
- The classifier ranks selected route changes, fallback status changes, backend reachability changes, selected model readiness changes, warning/error code changes, recommended action changes, and loaded model count changes ahead of generic JSON churn.
- Human and JSON output use the same classified change list and severity ordering.
- JSON output preserves machine-readable change type, subject, before value, after value, severity or rank, and explanation.
- Human output concisely explains the highest-impact changes.
- The command does not compare prompt outputs, evaluate model quality, infer answer correctness, or claim that an agent got better or worse.
- Tests cover route change, fallback introduced or removed, backend reachability change, readiness change, warning/error code changes, timestamp-only changes being ignored or deprioritized, malformed input, and incompatible snapshot schema versions.
notes:
2026-06-30 CLOSED:
COMPLETED:
- Implemented inferctl diff --before/--after for deterministic file-to-file control-plane snapshot comparison.
- Supports raw snapshot JSON and envelope-wrapped snapshots.
- Validates missing, unreadable, malformed, and incompatible snapshot inputs with clear errors.
- Uses the shared domain classifier for selected route, fallback, backend reachability, readiness, warning/error code, recommended action, and loaded-model-count changes.
- Human and JSON output share the same ranked change list.
- Added schema, capabilities, generated verb docs, contract golden, and tests for required change classes and validation paths.
- go test ./... and scripts/check-contract-goldens.sh passed before close.
BLOCKERS:
- none.
close_reason: Added inferctl diff --before/--after with validated snapshot inputs, ranked domain changes, JSON/human output, contract/docs/golden coverage, and tests — commit 9bd5f0e

title: Implement snapshot command
description: Drift debugging needs a durable artifact that captures inferctl's relevant control-plane view for a task at a point in time. Ad hoc doctor and route JSON files are useful while prototyping, but they do not define a comparable bundle format or privacy policy that future diff logic can rely on.
design: Add `inferctl snapshot --task <task>` with prompt-source support aligned to the shared prompt metadata contract. Persist a purpose-built normalized bundle rather than nested raw command envelopes. Support stdout output by default and `--output <snapshot.json>` for writing a requested path. Ship this with or after deterministic file-to-file diff so the snapshot schema is proven by comparison behavior before it becomes durable.
acceptance_criteria:
- `inferctl snapshot` is registered in the CLI, capabilities contract, schema export, generated verb docs, and contract goldens.
- Snapshot data includes an explicit snapshot schema version.
- Snapshot data includes task, prompt metadata, route decision, route candidates, backend reachability, loaded and installed model summaries, warnings/errors, inferctl version, contract version, and capture timestamp.
- The persisted artifact avoids nested raw command envelopes while reusing existing command data shapes where appropriate.
- Prompt content is redacted by default; the MVP stores only metadata such as source kind, character count, estimated token count, content hash, and filename or basename when relevant.
- `--output <snapshot.json>` writes the artifact to the requested path; without `--output`, the command writes the artifact to stdout.
- Snapshot creation is read-only and does not run inference, warm models, load models, mutate config, or write recurring state.
- Tests cover JSON shape, prompt redaction, output-file writing, unreadable prompt file, invalid config, deterministic mode, and compatibility with the file-to-file diff parser.
notes:
2026-06-30 CLOSED:
COMPLETED:
- Implemented inferctl snapshot --task with prompt-file, inline prompt, stdin, --output, and --json support.
- Raw snapshot artifacts include snapshot schema version, task, prompt metadata, route decision/candidates, backend reachability, installed and loaded model summaries, warnings/errors, inferctl version, contract version, and capture timestamp.
- Prompt text and local prompt paths are redacted by default; prompt hash and basename are stored when a prompt source is provided.
- --output writes a raw comparable artifact; --json emits the standard envelope while still writing raw output artifacts when requested.
- Added schema, capabilities, generated verb docs, contract golden, deterministic timestamp support, and diff compatibility tests.
- go test ./... and scripts/check-contract-goldens.sh passed before close.
BLOCKERS:
- none.
close_reason: Added inferctl snapshot with raw comparable artifacts, metadata-only prompt capture, backend/model control-plane state, envelope mode, docs/contracts/goldens, and diff compatibility tests — commit d83310d

title: Add snapshot retention and since lookup
description: Relative-date diff lookup and automatic snapshot history are useful, but they require explicit storage, retention, and privacy policy. Implementing them before the deterministic file-to-file flow would prematurely freeze recurring state under inferctl-managed storage.
design: Design and implement snapshot storage only after the file-to-file diff surface and explicit snapshot artifact are stable. Define where recurring snapshots are stored, what retention policy applies, how prompt metadata privacy is preserved, and how `inferctl diff --since <relative-date>` resolves the correct baseline snapshot.
acceptance_criteria:
- Automatic or recurring snapshot storage has an explicit storage location, retention policy, and privacy policy.
- `inferctl diff --since <relative-date>` resolves comparable stored snapshots using the same classifier as file-to-file diff.
- Stored snapshots continue to redact prompt content by default.
- The implementation handles missing history, multiple candidate snapshots, malformed stored snapshots, and incompatible snapshot schema versions with clear errors.
- Tests cover retention behavior, relative-date lookup, missing baseline, multiple baseline candidates, malformed stored snapshot, and incompatible schema version.
notes:
2026-06-30 CLOSED:
COMPLETED:
- Added explicit snapshot --store retained history with INFERCTL_SNAPSHOT_DIR override and default user state directory fallback.
- Added per-task --retention-limit pruning; default keeps 20 snapshots per task.
- Added diff --task <task> --since <relative> to resolve a stored baseline and compare it to a fresh read-only control-plane snapshot using the existing classifier.
- Stored snapshots remain raw comparable artifacts and use the existing metadata-only prompt redaction policy.
- Added handling/tests for retention pruning, relative lookup, missing baseline, multiple candidate baselines, malformed stored snapshots, and incompatible schema versions.
- go test ./... and scripts/check-contract-goldens.sh passed before close.
BLOCKERS:
- none.
close_reason: Added explicit snapshot --store retention, INFERCTL_SNAPSHOT_DIR storage policy, per-task pruning, and diff --task/--since lookup with validation tests — commit 82f74f3

title: Stabilize live-state contracts
description: External scripts need stable inferctl JSON surfaces for deciding which local backend and model to use, where that backend is reachable, whether it is healthy, and which models are exposed. Earlier demo plans confused `inferctl capabilities --json` with live machine state; the stable contract must make `route`, `config show`, `doctor`, and `models` dependable for tool builders without exposing secrets.
acceptance_criteria:
- `inferctl route <task> --json` exposes selected backend, selected model, fallback status, candidate list, per-candidate unavailability reasons, and a human-readable decision reason.
- `inferctl config show --json` maps backend names to `base_url` without exposing secret values in default output.
- `inferctl doctor --json` exposes backend reachability, warnings, loaded-model counts, and recommended action.
- `inferctl models --json` exposes model name, backend, and per-row readiness fields suitable for single-shot external checks.
- Contract goldens exercise the fields external scripts depend on.
- Demo and README guidance make clear that `capabilities --json` describes inferctl's binary contract, not live backend state.
notes:
2026-06-30 CLOSED:
COMPLETED:
- route contract fields covered by Tier 1 golden assertions and fallback route fixture (inferctl-np8.4, commit 07f1474).
- config show maps backend base_url while omitting secret-bearing fields (inferctl-np8.1, commit aa120c8).
- doctor recommended_action remains covered by contract fixture assertion (inferctl-np8.4, commit 07f1474).
- models rows expose loaded/available readiness fields (inferctl-np8.2, commit e6c9926).
- planning docs and README clarify capabilities as binary contract, not live state (inferctl-np8.3, inferctl-np8.5, commit 89bfb23).
VERIFICATION:
- All five child issues are closed.
- go test ./... passed during child verification.
- scripts/check-contract-goldens.sh passed during child verification.
BLOCKERS:
- none.
close_reason: Tier 1 live-state contract stabilization complete: config redaction aa120c8, model row state e6c9926, golden coverage 07f1474, docs 89bfb23, planning audit completed

title: Redact config show secrets
description: External scripts need to read `inferctl config show --json` for backend endpoint metadata, but default output currently exposes secret values such as `auth_header_value`. Tool-builder-facing JSON cannot become stable while it leaks credentials in the same envelope consumers are expected to pin against.
design: Change the default `config show --json` serializer so secret-bearing fields are omitted or redacted. If secret visibility is still required for a narrow diagnostic path, put it behind an explicit opt-in such as `--show-secrets` and keep default output safe for demos, editor config generation, and automation.
acceptance_criteria:
- Default `config show --json` output does not include plaintext `auth_header_value` or equivalent credential values.
- The output still includes `effective_config.backends.<name>.base_url` for configured backends.
- A golden under `testdata/contract/` asserts secret fields are absent from default output.
- Any opt-in secret display path is explicit and does not affect the default envelope.
notes:
2026-06-30 CLOSED:
COMPLETED:
- Default config show output redacts secret-bearing config fields before section/key selection.
- Secret-bearing provenance entries are omitted from default config show output.
- Tests cover preserving backend base_url while omitting auth_header_value from command output and the contract golden.
VERIFICATION:
- go test ./cmd/inferctl -run 'TestConfigShow|TestConfig' passed.
- go test ./internal/contract passed.
- scripts/check-contract-goldens.sh passed.
BLOCKERS:
- none.
close_reason: Redacted config show secret-bearing fields while preserving backend base_url; added command and contract golden coverage — commit aa120c8

title: Add loaded model rows
description: Team requirements verification and editor config generation need a single-shot `models --json` view that identifies which models are ready, without cross-referencing route candidates or backend-specific loaded-model structures.
design: Extend `models --json` row data with `loaded: bool` and `available: bool` where inferctl can know those states. Populate the fields from existing loaded-model detection per backend and keep the row shape consistent across supported backend kinds.
acceptance_criteria:
- `models --json` rows include `loaded: bool`.
- `models --json` rows include `available: bool` where that distinction is applicable.
- Fields are populated from existing backend loaded-model detection rather than from inference probes or warmup calls.
- Contract goldens pin the row shape and representative loaded/available values.
notes:
2026-06-30 CLOSED:
COMPLETED:
- models --json now emits dedicated rows with loaded and available booleans.
- Installed rows are marked available and loaded when existing loaded-model detection reports the same model.
- --loaded rows are marked loaded and available.
- Contract golden and CLI tests pin representative loaded/available row shape.
VERIFICATION:
- go test ./cmd/inferctl -run 'TestBackendsModelsAndModelCommands|TestEmptyListPayloadsUseArrays|TestLocalBackendAdaptersAreFirstClassKinds' passed.
- go test ./internal/contract -run 'TestModelsGoldenIncludesLoadedState|TestVerbGoldens' passed.
- scripts/check-contract-goldens.sh passed.
- go test ./... passed.
BLOCKERS:
- none.
close_reason: Added loaded and available booleans to models --json rows with CLI and contract golden coverage — commit e6c9926

title: Audit capabilities misuse
description: Planning docs and demos have previously used `inferctl capabilities --json` as though it reported live backend inventory. That misunderstanding will keep reappearing unless the planning corpus is corrected or explicitly annotated where capabilities introspection is deliberate.
design: Review `examples-and-demos-brainstorm.md` and the per-idea docs under `marketing/demos/`. For each reference to `capabilities --json`, decide whether it is introspecting inferctl's command contract or incorrectly trying to answer live-state questions. Replace live-state misuse with the relevant live primitives: `route`, `config show`, `doctor`, or `models`.
acceptance_criteria:
- `examples-and-demos-brainstorm.md` is reviewed for `capabilities --json` usage.
- Every per-idea markdown doc under `marketing/demos/` is reviewed for `capabilities --json` usage.
- Remaining `capabilities --json` references are either corrected to live-state primitives or annotated as deliberate command-contract introspection with a reason.
- No demo plan instructs external scripts to use `capabilities --json` to discover current backend reachability, loaded models, or route selection.
notes:
2026-06-30 CLOSED:
COMPLETED:
- Reviewed examples-and-demos-brainstorm.md and every markdown file under marketing/demos/.
- Corrected the brainstorm's agent stub and editor config generator examples to use route --json plus config show --json for live backend/model selection and endpoint metadata.
- Remaining capabilities --json references in the scoped planning corpus are deliberate annotations: command-contract introspection, compatibility checks, or explicit warnings that capabilities is not live state.
VERIFICATION:
- rg over the scoped planning corpus shows no remaining instruction to use capabilities --json for backend reachability, loaded models, or route selection.
- Parent planning directory is not a git worktree, so the brainstorm edit has no implementation commit; this tracker close records the completion.
BLOCKERS:
- none.
close_reason: Audited planning corpus and corrected brainstorm live-state misuse; no implementation commit because the edited planning file is outside this git repository

title: Cover Tier 1 goldens
description: The Tier 1 live-state contract is only stable if contract snapshots exercise the fields external scripts rely on. Existing goldens may catch accidental envelope changes but still miss individual fields if they are absent from representative fixtures.
design: Inspect the goldens under `testdata/contract/` for `route`, `config show`, `doctor`, and `models`. Add or adjust fixtures so each required field appears in at least one meaningful snapshot, including fallback and unavailable-candidate route cases where needed.
acceptance_criteria:
- A route contract golden exercises `decision.reason`.
- A route contract golden exercises `decision.is_fallback`.
- A route contract golden exercises per-candidate `unavailability_reason`.
- A `config show` contract golden exercises `effective_config.backends.<name>.base_url` without secret values.
- A doctor contract golden exercises `recommended_action`.
- A models contract golden exercises per-row `loaded` after the models row work lands.
notes:
2026-06-30 CLOSED:
COMPLETED:
- route.golden now exercises fallback decision reason, is_fallback, and an unavailable primary candidate with unavailability_reason.
- Contract tests assert route, config show, doctor, and models goldens retain the Tier 1 fields.
- Prior config show and models fixtures cover base_url-without-secret and per-row loaded/available state.
VERIFICATION:
- go test ./internal/contract -run 'TestConfigShowGoldenRedactsSecrets|TestModelsGoldenIncludesLoadedState|TestRouteGoldenCoversFallbackCandidateFields|TestDoctorGoldenIncludesRecommendedAction|TestVerbGoldens|TestContractGoldensUsePortablePathPlaceholders' passed.
- scripts/check-contract-goldens.sh passed.
- jq empty on Tier 1 fixtures passed.
- go test ./... passed.
BLOCKERS:
- none.
close_reason: Added Tier 1 golden assertions and fallback route fixture coverage for required live-state fields — commit 07f1474

title: Document tool-builder pattern
description: External consumers writing integrations against inferctl need an obvious README pattern for combining control-plane route decisions with configured endpoint metadata. Without that guidance, new demos and tool builders are likely to repeat the `capabilities --json` live-state mistake.
design: Add a README section for tool builders that describes the two-call pattern: use `inferctl route <task> --json` for the live backend/model decision and `inferctl config show --json` for the backend `base_url`. Include a Python snippet that calls inferctl for control-plane data, then makes a direct OpenAI-compatible data-plane request using the selected backend and model.
acceptance_criteria:
- README contains a "For tool builders" or equivalent section.
- The section explains `route --json` plus `config show --json` as the live backend-selection pattern.
- The section includes a Python snippet that reads the route decision, reads backend config, derives `base_url`, and calls an OpenAI-compatible client with the selected model.
- The section states that `capabilities --json` describes inferctl's binary command contract, not live backend state.
- The guidance reminds consumers to respect inferctl endpoint policy such as non-loopback `remote_allowed` handling.
notes:
2026-06-30 CLOSED:
COMPLETED:
- Added README For Tool Builders section.
- Documented route --json plus config show --json as the live backend-selection pattern.
- Added Python OpenAI-compatible handoff snippet using selected_backend, selected_model, and backend base_url.
- Clarified capabilities --json is for the binary command contract, not live backend state.
- Added remote_allowed endpoint-policy reminder for non-loopback openai_compat URLs.
VERIFICATION:
- Reviewed README section against all acceptance criteria.
BLOCKERS:
- none.
close_reason: Added README tool-builder handoff pattern for route/config show and direct OpenAI-compatible calls — commit 89bfb23
